events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts lack required bonding to the grounding electrode system, creating potential for energized metal and shock/arc hazard.",
    "Equipment bonding not in compliance with NEC 250.104 for bonding of metal parts and 250.30 grounding/bonding requirements for separately derived systems."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work in affected area and de-energize/isolate circuits until bonding is corrected.",
    "Install approved bonding jumpers connecting isolated metal parts to the equipment grounding conductor/grounding electrode system per NEC 250.104 and 250.30.",
    "Verify continuity of bonding path with appropriate testing before re-energizing.",
    "Notify electrical inspector/foreman and document corrective action."
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts in a commercial electrical installation must be bonded to ensure fault current has a low-impedance path and metal remains at safe potential. Missing bonding violates NEC 250.104/250.30 and presents a high shock/arc hazard.",
  "references": ["NEC 250.104", "NEC 250.30"]
}
